description_long: This is a large, double-edged battle axe with a broad, sweeping blade and a secondary, smaller lower blade forming a crescent shape. The main blade is thick and robust, with a circular central hub that appears reinforced, possibly for added strength or decorative effect. The handle is cylindrical, featuring a textured grip with segmented bands and capped at both ends with pointed, conical pommels. The axe head is mounted securely atop the handle, with the blade extending symmetrically on both sides. The entire object has a uniform, metallic appearance, suggesting it is forged from steel or iron. There are no visible engravings, markings, or color variations, giving it a utilitarian yet imposing look.
description: A large, double-edged metallic battle axe with a broad, sweeping blade and a textured cylindrical handle. The design is robust and symmetrical, with a reinforced central hub and pointed pommel caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full profile of the broad, double-edged blade and the circular central hub.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical handle, with the axe appearing almost as a straight line.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the symmetrical blade and handle design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the handle's length.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, straight handle with a textured grip and segmented bands, metallic material, capped with pointed pommels.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, broad sweeping blade with a crescent lower edge, reinforced circular central hub, symmetrical and robust geometry.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted over the handle and reinforced at the hub.
